However, based on the closest match, "Adina angulata" is a type of plant species, but I couldn't find any relevant information on "Adicea angulata". If you meant "Adina cordifolia", it is a plant species known for its medicinal properties. Here is a paragraph on "Adina cordifolia":

Adina cordifolia, commonly known as the "Heart-Leaf", is a type of plant species in the Rubiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Adina cordifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Adina cordifolia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used to make teas, tonics, and other herbal preparations.
